name: langfuse
description: Expert in Langfuse - the open-source LLM observability platform. Covers tracing, prompt management, evaluation, datasets, and integration with LangChain, LlamaIndex, and OpenAI. Essential for debugging, monitoring, and improving LLM applications in production. Use when: langfuse, llm observability, llm tracing, prompt management, llm evaluation.
capabilities: Instrument LLM calls with Langfuse tracing for OpenAI, LangChain, and LlamaIndex
Configure prompt management and versioning for iterative improvements
Set up evaluation pipelines to score and compare model outputs
Track token usage, costs, and latency across all LLM interactions
Create datasets for systematic testing and A/B testing of prompts
Debug production issues by analyzing traces, spans, and user sessions
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 17 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is documentation for Langfuse, an open-source LLM observability platform. External command detections are Python code examples, not shell execution. Network URLs are legitimate API endpoints. API key references are placeholder examples in documentation.
valueStatement: Debug and monitor your LLM applications with comprehensive tracing and evaluation. Langfuse provides open-source observability to track costs, latency, and quality across your AI workflows.
userTitle: Add LLM Observability and Tracing to Your AI Applications
limitations: Self-hosted deployment requires managing your own infrastructure
High-volume applications may need batch configuration tuning
Dashboard analytics may have slight latency for real-time monitoring
Evaluation scoring requires manual setup of criteria and thresholds

useCases.targetUser: Backend engineers deploying LLM features to production
useCases.title: Production LLM Application Monitoring
useCases.description: Track and debug live AI applications with full trace visibility. Monitor token costs, response latency, and error rates across user sessions.

useCases.targetUser: Prompt engineers iterating on AI behavior and responses
useCases.title: Prompt Development and Testing
useCases.description: Version prompts, compare outputs across iterations, and run A/B tests. Use datasets to systematically evaluate prompt changes before deployment.

useCases.targetUser: Engineering managers controlling AI infrastructure budgets
useCases.title: AI Cost and Performance Optimization
useCases.description: Analyze token usage patterns, identify expensive operations, and optimize model selection. Set up alerts for cost anomalies and performance degradation.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Tracing Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up Langfuse tracing for my OpenAI application. I need to track chat completions with user IDs and session IDs. Show me the Python code to initialize Langfuse and wrap my existing OpenAI calls.

promptTemplates.title: LangChain Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a LangChain application with custom chains and agents. Guide me through adding Langfuse callback handlers to trace all chain executions, including nested calls and tool usage.
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding observability to existing LangChain workflows

promptTemplates.title: Prompt Versioning and Comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to manage multiple versions of my system prompts in Langfuse and compare their performance. Show me how to create prompts in Langfuse, retrieve them in code, and analyze which versions perform better.
promptTemplates.scenario: Iterative prompt development with A/B testing

promptTemplates.title: Custom Evaluation Pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me build an evaluation pipeline that scores my LLM outputs based on relevance and accuracy. I need to create test datasets, define scoring criteria, and run batch evaluations to compare model versions.

bestPractices: Always call langfuse.flush() in serverless environments to ensure traces are sent before function exit
Include user_id and session_id on all traces to enable user-level debugging and session analytics
Use meaningful trace and span names that reflect business logic rather than generic function names
antiPatterns: Not flushing traces in serverless functions, causing data loss when execution ends
Tracing every operation without filtering, creating noise and performance overhead
Missing user and session identifiers, limiting ability to debug specific user experiences

faq.question: Is Langfuse free to use?
faq.answer: Langfuse offers a free tier for development and small-scale usage. Self-hosting is completely free and open-source. Cloud hosting has paid plans for higher volumes and additional features.

faq.question: Does Langfuse work with models other than OpenAI?
faq.answer: Yes. Langfuse supports OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, LlamaIndex, LangChain, and custom integrations. You can trace any LLM by using the manual tracing API with your own instrumentation.

faq.question: How do I handle sensitive data in traces?
faq.answer: Use the metadata masking feature to redact sensitive fields before sending to Langfuse. You can also self-host Langfuse to keep all trace data within your infrastructure.

faq.question: Can I use Langfuse with streaming responses?
faq.answer: Yes. Langfuse automatically handles streaming for supported integrations. For manual tracing, log the complete response after the stream finishes, or use incremental updates for long-running streams.

faq.question: How do I compare model performance across different providers?
faq.answer: Use Langfuse datasets to run the same test cases against different models. Tag traces with model names and use the dashboard to compare latency, cost, and quality scores side by side.

faq.question: What happens if Langfuse is unavailable during tracing?
faq.answer: The Langfuse SDK batches traces asynchronously and retries on failure. Your application continues normally even if Langfuse is temporarily unreachable. Traces are queued and sent when connectivity resumes.
